location_of_combat_zone.location_of_combat_zone: Iraq – Al Anbar Province (Fallujah and Ramadi areas)
location_of_combat_zone.details_and_time_period: U.S. Marine Corps deployment during Operation Iraqi Freedom. Primary combat zone assignments in and around Fallujah and Ramadi from 2004-03 to 2004-10, with frequent mounted and dismounted patrols, route security, and urban operations. Subsequent shorter rotation back through Al Asad Air Base in western Al Anbar in 2005-06 for follow‑on security operations and training of Iraqi Security Forces.

Attached to a joint task force element based out of Baghdad International Airport (BIAP) and the Victory Base Complex from approximately 2005-10 to 2006-03. Conducted convoy security along MSR Tampa and various supply routes, as well as perimeter security, quick reaction force (QRF) duties, and liaison with Army units operating in the greater Baghdad area.

Operation Enduring Freedom deployment with a Marine infantry battalion from 2010-05 to 2011-01. Stationed primarily in Helmand Province (near Sangin and Kajaki districts), conducting counter‑insurgency operations, partnered patrols with Afghan National Army units, and security for combat engineer route‑clearance missions. Exposure to frequent IED threats, indirect fire, and small‑arms engagements.

location_of_combat_zone.location_of_combat_zone: Kuwait – Staging area in support of Iraq operations
location_of_combat_zone.details_and_time_period: Non‑combat staging and transit through Camp Doha and later Camp Arifjan, Kuwait, in support of Operation Iraqi Freedom, from approximately 2004-02 to 2004-03 and again for re‑deployment processing in late 2004 and early 2006. While designated as a hazardous duty station, the decedent’s primary role here was pre‑deployment training, equipment staging, and onward movement coordination rather than direct combat operations.

location_of_combat_zone.location_of_combat_zone: Persian Gulf – Naval and littoral waters
location_of_combat_zone.details_and_time_period: Embarked briefly on U.S. Navy vessels transiting the Persian Gulf in 2005-09 and again in 2010-04 as part of movement between Kuwait and operational areas in Iraq and Afghanistan. Duties included force protection details on board ship, weapons maintenance, and readiness drills. While not engaged in direct naval combat, this period is recognized as service in the Persian Gulf combat zone for tax and VA purposes.

details_and_time_period.details_and_time_period: N/A – decedent was born in 1981 and did not serve during World War II.
N/A – decedent’s service years (2002–2014) were well after the Korean War period of 1950–1955.
N/A – decedent never served in Vietnam; his active duty service was during the post‑9/11 era.
N/A – no deployments to the Horn of Africa or Operation Restore Hope/UNOSOM II era; all overseas deployments were to Iraq, Afghanistan, Kuwait, and nearby waters.
N/A – decedent entered service after the primary U.S. combat and peacekeeping operations in Bosnia and Kosovo had significantly scaled down.

details_and_time_period.location_of_combat_zone: Global War on Terrorism – Other locations
details_and_time_period.details_and_time_period: Aside from Iraq, Afghanistan, Kuwait, and brief time in the Persian Gulf, the decedent did not deploy to other named GWOT locations such as Djibouti, Syria, or the Philippines.

check_if_served.details_and_time_period: Multiple combat tours between 2004 and 2006. Initial tour with 1st Marine Division in Al Anbar Province, followed by a second deployment attached to a joint task force operating out of Baghdad. Duties included infantry squad leader responsibilities, convoy security, and coordination with Army and coalition partners. Received Combat Action Ribbon for engagement with enemy forces during an ambush near Fallujah in mid‑2004.

Single extended deployment from 2010-05 to 2011-01 in Helmand Province. Served as a staff sergeant overseeing a squad of Marines conducting partnered patrols with Afghan National Army units, route security, and village stability operations. Incurred mild traumatic brain injury from an IED blast in late 2010 but returned to duty after evaluation.

Several weeks before and after each Iraq deployment (2004 and 2005–2006) spent in Kuwait for in‑processing, pre‑deployment training, equipment staging, and re‑deployment out‑processing. Although not a primary combat assignment, this time is recognized as deployment in direct support of combat operations.

check_if_served.location_of_combat_zone: Persian Gulf (naval transit and operations area)
check_if_served.details_and_time_period: Short durations aboard Navy transport and amphibious ships transiting the Persian Gulf, supporting movement of personnel and equipment to and from Iraq and Kuwait. Participated in shipboard force protection watches and readiness drills.

veterans_status___location_of_combat_zon.name_any_other_locations_in_this_space: Kuwait (Camp Doha and Camp Arifjan) – used as staging and logistics hubs in direct support of Operations Iraqi Freedom and Enduring Freedom. Time there included intensive pre‑deployment training in desert conditions, weapons qualification ranges, convoy rehearsal exercises, and coordination with logistics units for onward movement into Iraq. Although engagements with enemy forces did not occur in these camps, the decedent often mentioned that the sense of danger was still present due to the proximity to active combat zones and the constant movement of wounded personnel and equipment through these facilities.
